name: Giant Walks
source: Chris Atkinson · Y3–Y4 long jump session card

ks1.objective: Rehearse the take-off shape — knee drive and opposite-arm swing — in slow motion.
successCriteria: Knee comes up to hip-height on at least 4 strides

lks2.objective: Slow-motion rehearsal of the take-off shape — knee drive plus opposite-arm action, tall body throughout.
successCriteria: Knee drives to hip-height every stride

uks2.objective: Slow-motion rehearsal of take-off coordination with full extension — knee drive, arm punch, hip mobility.
successCriteria: Full hip range on each knee drive
Holds tall posture without losing tempo

equipment.item: Marked lane (5–10 m) — use cones or floor markings

space.description: A 5–10 m lane, grass or indoor floor. Wide enough for one child at a time, or several lanes side-by-side. Open space behind for walking back.

steps: Line children up at one end of the lane — one per lane, or queue if one lane.
On the coach's "go", they walk forward with EXAGGERATED strides.
Each stride: drive the knee UP HIGH (hip-height or more), opposite arm forward, body TALL.
Walk SLOWLY — this is a shape rehearsal, not a race. 1 stride per second.
Reach the end, turn around, walk back at normal pace.
Repeat once for a total of 45 seconds across both directions.

errors.cause: Rushing — moving the feet faster than the legs lift
errors.fix: Slow it down explicitly. "Show me how HIGH your knee can go." Call out specific children doing it well.
Cue eyes up: "Eyes on the end of the lane." Tall posture comes from the head.
Pause. March on the spot first. Get the cross-pattern there. THEN move forward.

progressions: Standard: 5–10 m lane at walking pace, exaggerated knee + arm.
Tighter lane forces tighter strides — adds control challenge.
Hold the knee at the top for 1 second on each stride — tests balance.
Add a clap on each knee lift — adds rhythm.
Strong: eyes-closed version with a spotter — proprioception.
questions: Which ARM is forward when your right knee comes up?
What stays STILL when your knee comes up?
Why do we lift the knee high?
base.showmanship: Call it "slow-motion superhero walks". Mime the exaggerated version yourself before they start — kids buy in faster when the coach commits to looking silly first.
